page: Microsoft Azure D64ns_v6 is a network-optimized, general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dnv6 family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor with an x86_64 architecture, featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 32 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor operates at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz and includes a 260 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, but it is premium storage capable.

Benchmark data reveals strong performance characteristics across multiple domains. The CPU demonstrates robust capabilities in extended instructions and encryption, as evidenced by strong Passmark CPU scores. In memory-bound operations, the server achieves a strong read-only small-block cached memory bandwidth of 4125.39 GB/sec, though large-block uncached read and write bandwidths remain average. Notably, the instance excels in static web serving throughput, reaching a top-tier score of 90.06 GB/sec. It also exhibits strong performance in database operations, Redis SET operations at 71.06 million operations per second, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression.

The Intel Xeon processor supports advanced instruction sets, including Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), which accelerate vector and matrix math. This hardware acceleration translates to strong performance in LLM inference tasks, particularly for prompt processing and text generation on small to medium models. Given its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, high-speed network optimization, and strong database and web serving benchmarks, the D64ns_v6 is well-suited for high-throughput web serving, database hosting, and medium-scale machine learning inference workloads.
description: The Microsoft Azure D64ns_v6 is a network-optimized, general-purpose virtual machine from the Dnv6 family. Powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz, it features 64 vCPUs (32 physical cores) and 256.0 GB of RAM, maintaining a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports AVX-512 and AMX instructions. While the server lacks local storage and GPUs, it is premium storage capable. In benchmark testing, the instance delivers top-tier static web serving throughput of 90.06 GB/sec, alongside strong performance in database operations, Redis SET operations, and multi-threaded decompression. It also demonstrates strong efficiency in LLM inference for small and medium models. This hardware profile makes the D64ns_v6 highly suitable for high-throughput web serving, database workloads, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64ns_v6 is a network-optimized general-purpose virtual machine featuring 64 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 256 GB of RAM, and advanced instruction support for database and web serving workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64ns_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine with 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and top-tier web serving performance.
tagline: A network-optimized virtual machine offering balanced compute and memory with advanced hardware acceleration for high-throughput web and database workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 64 dedicated vCPUs and 4.2 GHz clock speed
256 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Top-tier static web serving throughput of 90.06 GB/sec
Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512 for accelerated CPU-based inference
Optimized for high-throughput web serving, database hosting, and Redis deployments
